reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully encodes the informal statement. G is a finite group (`[Group G] [Fintype G]`), φ is a group automorphism (`G ≃* G` is MulEquiv, equivalent to an automorphism for groups), and the hypothesis that φ(x) = x⁻¹ for more than three-fourths of elements is captured via a Finset I with `(0.75 : ℚ) * card G < card I` and every x ∈ I satisfying φ(x) = x⁻¹. This encoding is equivalent to the informal claim: the set A = {x | φ(x) = x⁻¹} satisfies |A| ≥ |I| > (3/4)|G|, and conversely given |A| > (3/4)|G| we can take I = A. The strict inequality `<` correctly matches "more than three-fourths" (a companion counterexample in the file confirms the `≤` version would be false due to D₈). The conclusion `(∀ x, φ x = x⁻¹) ∧ (∀ x y, x*y = y*x)` faithfully expresses "φ(y) = y⁻¹ for all y, and G is abelian".
